title: Op result (command plane response)
description: Two-phase honesty: the intent audit (audit.op.intent, outcome: attempted) commits BEFORE dispatch; the completion audit (audit.op.outcome: ok|failed|executor_died) commits when the effect resolves, linked by the op id. `status: accepted` means the effect is in flight on an async executor (spool/RPC) — the UI renders in-progress, NOT success; completion arrives as the op's declared emits[] type carrying the op id. `status: applied` means the effect completed synchronously. Duplicate (principal, id) within the dedup window (>= 24h, >= max retry horizon) returns the recorded result; the same id with a DIFFERENT body is rejected (id_reused). Consumers MUST ignore unknown fields.

code.description: Stable snake_case: unauthorized, lane_denied, scope_denied, executor_unreachable, audit_unavailable, stale_fence, not_lease_holder, illegal_transition, claim_lost, id_reused, interrupt_ineligible, target_not_owned, ssrf_rejected, source_mismatch, rate_limited, ...

retryable.description: May the caller re-send the SAME id after backoff.

audit_seq.description: Lake seq of the INTENT audit emission — the receipt. Null in exactly three cases: unauthorized/lane_denied (the denial itself is separately audited for term.*), audit_unavailable (op did not run), and read ops (which skip audit by contract §5.1).

liveness.description: For spool-inlet executors (manager) this is the manager's heartbeat freshness — the honest availability signal.
